Gauntlets of Fiery Might  
 


Slot: HANDS
AC: 10
STR: +7 DEX: +7 STA: +7
SV DISEASE: +7 SV POISON: +7
WT: 0.3 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD MNK BRD ROG BST BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Gauntlets of Fiery Might

Slot: HANDS
AC: 10
STR: +7 DEX: +7 STA: +7
SV DISEASE: +7 SV POISON: +7
WT: 0.3 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD MNK BRD ROG BST BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Chain
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):50, 50, 50
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:30 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:1260
Submitted By:Kaine
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-14 02:54:34
Page Updated:Sun Apr 23rd, 2017

Expansion: Original
Average Price: No Data Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 45

Gloves 1/17
QuoteReply
# Jan 17 2003 at 4:58 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592256216339
5 posts
So after my 11th pickup raid with Ayonae's Dragonslayers I FINALLY win a lotto, and its these gloves. At this point in time I'm pretty happy and its hard to find gloves with these stats if you're not in a high raiding guild. You can keep your SCHW, these are mine :)


P.S. 4k on ayonae gimme tell =p

Not bad
QuoteReply
# Jun 27 2002 at 12:43 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591751437183

Scholar
151 posts
I wouldn't mind a pair of these to replace my Gnome Skin gloves.... Nice AC, and with the dual Trance Sticks I'm usually swinging, I don't mind the extra Dex one bit, either :)

Considering that my guild's working up a raid on the ol' Nag tonight (if he pops on-time and some earlybird 6am guild doesn't beat us to him), maybe I'll get lucky. Hope my dice are hot tonight, heheheh.

QuoteReply
# Apr 29 2002 at 4:55 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591767079068

266 posts
Was in a pickup raid on rodcet nife, and we took down naggy. had 7 groups. Only needed about 20 rezzes (hehe). 3 of these dropped and one of those weight reducing bags. Nothing they'd even let the clerics in on for a roll :(

So much for pickup raids.

# May 10 2002 at 2:24 PM Rating: Default

You're more inclined to get non-class specific loot (IE, a caster getting a CoF or these gauntlets) on a pickup raid that a guild/organized raid.

#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 29 2002 at 5:03 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) these are very nice monk gaunts. i wouldn't take these over CF's (obviously) but if u can manage to get yur hands on these b4 55+ (when you'd usually get epics) they great!

# Aug 20 2002 at 2:23 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966211935
41 posts
1) you are an idiot these gaunts are nowhere near as good as CF's (monk epic)
2) your profile is fake, notice your BP is a gm issued bp, right click "guide health" thx newb, and if you're gunna fake that **** at least make your monk better i don't know anyone who has tov loot and uses bracelet of quickness/might, thx
3) your keyring is stupid as hell, you don't even have sebilis key, and if you wanna look uber put a vex thal key in there, thx next time research your **** before you try to act cool.

Edit: LMFAO after looking into you since you had 97 posts of BS, i found your real monk: https://everquest.allakhazam.com/db/userinfo.html?char=192073 kthx newb.

▲ Page top
RE: gaunts
QuoteReply
# Dec 16 2003 at 1:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1702858585426
Scholar
633 posts
You call this person an "idiot" and demonstrate even if so, s/he is not alone. Katris says very unoffensively these are "very nice for a monk" and then goes on to say s/he would "not take them over CF's (obviously)". S/he doesn't say they are anywhere near as good, yet you flame on and publicly call him names and throw in, offensive to many, expleatives. After your own seemingly inability to read you rant on and on off topic about his profile. If a sysadmin falls in the forest do any users care? Of course not, after all they are "users". But trees burn very well.
Anyway, many a growing monk can find themselves in a situation of having upgraded their haste from SCHWs and being in a pre CF predicament in the hand slot due to the limited availability of dropable light weight items with stats as good or better than SCHW's. So many come here looking at gloves like these, and opinions of others to help them make descisions.

I got a pair of these for my monk on a recent GM Event In DL - Lady Draconis was there shouting that she needed help slaying a droagon, then it popped.. ?
QuoteReply
# Oct 07 2001 at 10:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251408865
12 posts
I was just wondering if anyone could explain why these are such good gauntlets? I just bought Gauntlets of the Black for 550pp - these go for (if the post is anywhere close) 19kpp. Gauntlets of the Black have 5 more armor class than these but it doesn't have quite the stats. I'm not so sure that I would drop 5ac on my Pally for these gloves, even if I had 19k burning a hole in my pocket. Anyway I was just wondering why these are such great things to have... is it simply the rarity?

# Oct 09 2001 at 3:42 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762612188
111 posts

I would let 5ac go for this gloves...no question.

Combat value of these...all things considered..is higher than GoB.

Decent ac...str, dx, sta + nice RESIST nice weigh next to nothing good for me.

I could even drop that silly ruby veil and replace with snanck(sp?) skin mask to make-up ac AND add more str.

However, I would not pay for them...nope...but I would take them over GoB in a sec if offered.

Amosk

50th level monk's opinion
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2001 at 12:00 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Prior to the existence of the monk epic (Celestial Fists), I would have said that this is definitely a monk item, BUT now that we have CF's, I have to say that argument has been weakened. I am not saying it should NEVER go to a monk, but it is no longer the ultimate monk gloves. Odds are that by the time you get a pair of these you will almost have your epic.
▲ Page top
RE: 50th level monk's opinion
QuoteReply
# Jul 31 2001 at 11:36 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
CF's aren't the Ultimate Monk gloves anymore, either- there are plenty of high AC, high stat gaunt's available in Velious.

I'd take Gauntlets of Dragonslaying and dual weild priceless and primal hammers, and get a chanter buff to reach the haste cap, for most situations.

I'm a 54 Disciple on a newer server, and you can bet your butt I'll be rolling on these in the meantime- if Naggy or Sev would ever cough them up, that is.
▲ Page top
RE: 50th level monk's opinion
QuoteReply
# Oct 26 2001 at 3:05 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965965331
52 posts
I am two fights from my epic (YOU WILL NOT EVADE ME, XENOVORASH) and must say I would not trade my epic for the Rubber Glove of Veeshan Proctology, or any other crazee ToV dropped gloves out there... you just can't beat the sweet clicky on them, and clicking is fun to boot.

CFs are a very nice pair of gloves as is, and the effect makes them one of the best epics.

I have YET to see a monk who no longer wears his. Heck, I don't think monks even get consideration for higher level glove drops.

PS: Bloody sig, I'm 56, 56 dammit!
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 03 2001 at 2:31 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) once again if you help kill the mob you are intitled to your fare chance at a roll
GoFM
QuoteReply
# Jun 25 2001 at 5:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248017854
3 posts
These are usually for Monks and Rogues to roll on. The other classes get better, easier. Please don&#8217;t even argue this if you are pre 50. These boards are always filled with newbies arguing over loot they wont even see for a LONG time.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 17 2001 at 8:27 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I dont get it, call me a n00be or what ever but why are monks so concerd about weight, so is every other class, I have a warrior if he goes over weight his stats drop too. I have never played a monk so I dont realy know can some one fill me in plz. monk guants my foot, they are great for all the classes that can wear them, come on look at those stats, try not to drool )
RE: i dont get it
QuoteReply
# Jun 19 2001 at 7:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249238742
18 posts
Before the flames come =)

Monks are concerened with weight because no matter what their strength is, they have a weight cap that even at lvl 60 does not exceed 20 stone.

Oh sure they can cary more but their AC drops like nobody's business for every stone over their cap. This is a very major issue for monks and limits the equipment that they can use effectively.

Kexel Spineweaver
Monk of the 27th Fist
Saryrn Server
▲ Page top
RE: i dont get it
QuoteReply
# Jul 13 2001 at 2:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249478566
Scholar
84 posts
oops

at lvl 51, it's 18

▲ Page top
RE: i dont get it
QuoteReply
# Jul 13 2001 at 2:48 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592249478566
Scholar
84 posts
Actually at level 60, the max is 24 stones. It goes up 1 stone for every 15 levels till you get over 51 then you get a slight additional bonus. Here's the table.

lvl 1 - 14
lvl 15 - 15
lvl 30 - 16
lvl 45 - 17
lvl 51 - 17
lvl 55 - 20
lvl 60 - 24

Sslythr
Lvl 38 Iksar Monk
Lanys T'Vyl


▲ Page top
RE: i dont get it
QuoteReply
# May 26 2002 at 12:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761526283
82 posts
Actually from lv55 up you can carry 25 stone without losing any AC. I've yet to test this since im not quite 55, but give me a little bit and I'll report back. I've talked to many 55+ monks and they say at 55 you can carry 25 stone, so thats what Im going by atm.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 19 2001 at 7:07 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) if it were an item meant for monks only, then it would have been listed as monks ONLY. if yer a class that can use, and the gaunts are better than what ye have on, ye should be allowed to roll on them, end of story.
#Anonymous, Posted: May 02 2001 at 11:30 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Any of the classes listed above could make good use of this, if I hear another monk ***** about losing a low weight item I'm going to scream... I mean, let's be honest, how much nice armor do rangers get regardless of weight? The stats are excellent for a rogue or warrior as well...
#Anonymous, Posted: May 15 2001 at 7:18 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I don't think a warrior should be able to role on these (maybe not even SK/Pally).
RE: sorry
QuoteReply
# Jul 21 2001 at 1:10 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592249163608
View User Forum
52 posts
Forget the AC, as an SK I'd want them to maintain stats (Str/Sta) when I switched to resist jewelery from my normal Stats ones.

If I'm pulling, I want resists more that AC, with these I wouldn't have to kill my melee ability to have good resists.
▲ Page top
Looting rights
QuoteReply
# Apr 30 2001 at 9:28 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592246284896
57 posts
Sorry but i think everyone should have the same chance to roll on any item that lists their class as able to use it.

Its stupid to only let monks roll for these as, yes they have low weight, but really ANY of the classes up there could benefit greatly from them.

Funny how on the forum for the RBB off of Vox only monks were allowed to roll on them for most raids also...

Still this is just my opinion but it seems to be the logical one.
▲ Page top
RE: Looting rights
QuoteReply
# Aug 21 2001 at 2:22 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249395162
97 posts
i'm in agreement also. if you say paladins get better items your right but i play to paladins and both have the same problem..weight! we have low str to start with so these would help us alot as well as any class that can use them for the reason of... str ups how much you hit for along with how good you are with useing the type of weapon, your offence. (say in these instinces:
96 str, 110 in 1hs, 110 offence, 15 dmg weapon = about 530 attack rateing.) its true becuase my paladin is only level 24 and has: 96 str, 110 in 1hs, 110 offence, 15 dmg weapon(GB) and has attack for over 530.
▲ Page top
RE: Looting rights
QuoteReply
# Jun 26 2001 at 5:10 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592756943443
17 posts
I agree...and I play a monk. On dragon loot the only pieces that should even be CONSIDERED to be monk only rolling on are the bags. (since other classes can use normal backpacks or an EE bag or something...and the not every monk under the sun can afford a tink bag)

I personally would love to have these gaunts for my monk. I've lost the roll on them each time I have seen them dropped dropped, but I have been happy for the person that won, and I continue to wear my treant fists. This isn't a monk only item.

A good amount of all/all or multi class useable stuff is and should be monk only rolling, but this is definately not one of those things, due solely to the work involved in killing one of these things. (all dragon loot is low weight anyhow)

Besides not every warrior has a pair of cobolt gloves, and these are a nice pair of gloves for ANY of the classes listed to go kill Trakanon or something like that
▲ Page top
RE: MONK GAUNTS ALL THE WAY
QuoteReply
# Apr 25 2001 at 8:14 PM Rating: Decent
